release_notes: Makes the Send Message card on the main window dynamically size to its content (the checkbox grid for online peers), instead of consuming all remaining vertical space below the Ready button. Previously the card was packed with fill='both', expand=True — so when a station detected only one or two peers on the LAN there was a large empty void below the checkboxes, and when many peers were detected the card stayed pinned at the same height regardless. v1.0.40 flips the layout: the Send Message card now packs with fill='x' and grows/shrinks with its checkbox grid, while the top row (Station Setup + Ready Messages log) packs with fill='both', expand=True and absorbs whatever vertical space the Send Message card releases. Net effect: roughly consistent padding around the checkbox grid no matter how many peers are visible, and the Ready Messages log automatically gets taller when there are few peers (more room for messages). Also adds a small pady=6 under the targets grid so the visible space below the last checkbox row roughly matches the space above the 'Send Message' header. Cross-platform — only Tk pack/grid changes, identical behavior on macOS and Windows.
